summary.note: Medium findings are terminology inconsistencies between hivemind-refactor (using 'Large Class', 'Long Method') and hivemind-patterns (using 'God Component', 'God Function') for equivalent anti-patterns. This does not block functionality but creates naming confusion.

key_findings: 2 medium-severity terminology inconsistencies: hivemind-refactor vs hivemind-patterns anti-pattern naming (Large Class/Long Method vs God Component/God Function)
All 7 skills have valid cross-references to sibling skills and use-hivemind-* domain routers
All referenced scripts exist and are valid executable bash scripts
All referenced templates and reference documents exist
Gate definitions between hivemind-gatekeeping and use-hivemind-context are complementary (different scope) - intentional design
carry_forward.recommended_next_action: Review terminology inconsistency between hivemind-refactor and hivemind-patterns. Consider either: (1) unifying anti-pattern names to use 'God Component/God Function' across both skills, or (2) documenting the equivalence mapping in code-smell-taxonomy.md to cross-reference the anti-pattern catalog
